description: Same style as entries-ambiguous-date.json (title dates 09/08, 08/07, 06/05, 05/04/2026, each component <= 12, strictly decreasing under both the MM/DD and DD/MM reading), except the third-newest entry is dated 17/06/2026 — the >12 component is FIRST, so §3.1's adopted DD/MM order differs from the provisional first-seen-wins MM/DD labelling data-model §3.1 step 1 assigns before resolution, making order ADOPTION observable rather than coincidentally identical to the provisional pick. Section headings match the rest of the set so the only cross-entry difference is the one §3.1's ambiguity pass resolves.

entries.content:
# 09/08/2026 — checkout: latency blip

## What happened
Checkout p95 latency spiked briefly during a routine scaling event.

## Timeline
Alert fired, on-call investigated, and latency recovered within six minutes.

## Resolution
No action was needed; the autoscaler caught up on its own.

## Follow-ups
Lower the autoscaler's warm-up threshold so the blip does not recur.

# 08/07/2026 — payments-api: brief error burst

## What happened
payments-api saw a short burst of elevated 5xx responses.

## Timeline
Alert fired, on-call investigated, and the error rate recovered within eight minutes.

## Resolution
The burst self-resolved once a downstream dependency's brief outage cleared.

## Follow-ups
Add a circuit breaker so a downstream blip does not surface as payments-api errors.

# 17/06/2026 — search-api: reindex latency

## What happened
search-api query latency briefly exceeded its budget during a reindex.

## Timeline
Alert fired, on-call investigated, and latency returned to budget once the reindex finished.

## Resolution
No action was needed beyond confirming the reindex was expected.

## Follow-ups
Schedule reindexes outside peak query hours.

# 06/05/2026 — inventory: count mismatch

## What happened
inventory reported a brief count mismatch during a warehouse feed replay.

## Timeline
Alert fired, on-call investigated, and counts matched once the replay completed.

## Resolution
No action was needed; the mismatch cleared once the replay caught up.

## Follow-ups
Suppress the mismatch alert while a known replay is in progress.

# 05/04/2026 — notifier: small backlog

## What happened
notifier queued a small backlog of low-priority notifications during a deploy.

## Timeline
Alert fired, on-call investigated, and the backlog drained within five minutes.

## Resolution
No action was needed; the backlog drained on its own after the deploy finished.

## Follow-ups
Exclude low-priority notifications from the backlog alert threshold.
